metadata.name: Chop Chop #108
metadata.description:
Chop Chop is a complementary collection to Caterpillars that I prepared to thank my collectors for the amazing love and interest you showed to my work. I did not want to force my way to your wallets with unsolicited thank-you nfts, so all holders of Caterpillars will have a reserve spot to mint at least 1 tokens per wallet for 0 tez ( I dont understand the reserve options fully, sorry - 128 editions reserved for holders of Caterpillars) . 

The holders of this collection will have reserve spots for my next  collection. The reserve spots will be held for one week from minting, and I prefer the unreserved editions to be fair game for anyone, so first come, first served.

I decided to re-work this older project of mine because it was going to take some time for me to prepare an independent and novel collection and also wanted to continue learning new things. The concept is Paul Klee meets Vector Math and Flow Fields, as you can see the visuals are heavily inspired by Paul Klee's work :)

Under the hood, the circles and triangles act as repulsive forces that break and distort the grid, and push the squares in their range out of their way, forming some holes in the fabric created by the same algo that made Caterpillars. 

I have only added one rarity feature which is the palette because this one uses almost all the palettes from Caterpillars (minus neon dreams). I am hoping that this project will not be about rarity, but more like a thank-you gift and an easy way to access my future work.

Well, I hope you will like it...

- Press "s" to save a png.

- Also, 33% royalty split wth the Processing Foundation, % 33 with the minters (if there will be any secondary sales) 

DISCLAIMER :
I am very very new to coding and not very good at this. I have tried to clean up the code as best as I could, and I hope that there is nothing terribly wrong left inside.

metadata.name: alchemy(maxima) #25
metadata.description:
This collection takes its main inspiration from the Turkish marbling art “Ebru” and tries to spin it with a golden twist, hence alchemy :) 

alchemy(maxima) tries to create a composition either using one of the two grid types, and sometimes breaking them or in a totally chaotic way, but uses color harmony and rhythm to keep the chaos in check and the resulting image still somewhat interesting and sometimes golden-ish…

The name includes “maxima” because this is the first part of a 2-part series; its companion collection “alchemy(minima)” will be released soon and as per my tradition will be freely available to maxima holders.

From the proceeds of the primary sales of alchemy(maxima),

- 33% will directly go to The Processing Foundation,
- 33% will fund the greater evil,
- 34% will be used to grow my collection.

So in a way, all the proceeds will go back into the ecosystem. This is my way of making up for taking tez out with my donations earlier.

If you are wondering what the hell “the greater evil” is, read about it here : 

https://twitter.com/evilnft/status/1544320804304961536?s=20&t=AoEfLfn32tGNR8JTi5Jjng

And find it here:

https://www.fxhash.xyz/u/the%20greater%20evil/collection

—

About the work: 

- 22 palettes - 7 new, 15 recycled,
- 3 main variants,
- 4 sub-variants each,
- 9 visible and some hidden features :)
- After going live, press “s” to save a png (at your screen resolution)
- No hi-res output due to the warp manipulating the pixel array directly, which means higher res equals to much higher computation time.

Thank you all in advance and have a good day!

Warp functions are based on this article by Inigo Quilez : https://iquilezles.org/articles/warp/

DISCLAIMER : I am not a good coder, I have tried to fix my past mistakes from my earlier projects to make some room for new mistakes :) I am learning on the way and certainly hoping that there aren't any huge bugs or anything, so you can enjoy your mint! Please hit me up if you have any concerns after minting and we can discuss a solution.

metadata.name: Hexaëder #141
metadata.description:
Hexaëder (cube) is a research into abstraction and a search for a balance between simplification and visual complexity.

No matter how simple and unambiguous things appear to be, in reality they are often more complex. Enjoy the simple, explore the complex.

Hexaeder has a wide variety of possible outcomes. I have put together 10 different color palettes; the number of cubes ranges from 10 to 401 (18 different grids); each cell can have 4 different background patterns; each cube can be filled in 4 distinct ways; there are 5 line colors and 16 different "cube figures"; the vignette can be black or be the color of the palette, and there are several "texture settings". This results in over 1.8 million possibilities.

For more information see: https://www.behance.net/gallery/131114761/Hexaeder-Flattened-cubes

metadata.name: Breeding Grounds #55
metadata.description:
Social Networks.
Molecular Clouds.
Neuronal Circuitries.
Quantum Mechanical Structures.

Blurring Boundaries.
Infinite Self-Similarities.

Total.
Mess.

Perfect.
Order.

/////
Each element (besides the painting ground textures) is based on the underlying subdivided grid which is sometimes exposed like some kind of preliminary drawing.

Note on the split:
After seeing some of the outputs, Sermin had a dream about “Breeding Grounds” and I decided to incorporate events and feelings from her dream into the project. That's how the names of the color palettes came about. Thank you for naming them, Sermin!

Press 's' to save a .png with the current resolution (2000x3000).
If you want a file with higher resolution and/or without the “Painting Ground Textures” for printing please contact me via Twitter or Discord. 

/////
Created with p5.js

Twitter: @serminmin / @pxlshrd

metadata.name: Bits Balanced #199
metadata.description:
Mathematically balanced structures inspired by pixel art.

Features:
1 - Toggle Border
S - Save

Query Parameters:
grain=false - turns off grain texture
resolution=2048 - sets resolution
frame=false - turns frame off

Credit:
Thanks to @Estienne_ca for the grainy texture algorithm
